Title: Book an appointment with valid patient, provider, service, location and future time slot
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler at a session location; an active patient, a bookable service, a provider and a location exist
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Appointment Scheduling app | 2. Search and select the active patient | 3. Click Add new appointment | 4. Select the service | 5. Select the provider | 6. Select the location | 7. Select a future date and an available time slot | 8. Set status Scheduled | 9. Click Save
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic (ID=10001V); Service=General Medicine; Provider=Dr A Tester; Location=Outpatient Clinic; Date=02-Jul-2026; Time=10:00
Expected_Result: Appointment is created with a unique appointment ID, status Scheduled, and appears on the patient's appointment list and the provider's day view with the selected service, location and time

Title: Booking is blocked when no patient is selected
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; on the Add new appointment screen with no patient context
Test_Steps: 1. Open Add new appointment without selecting a patient | 2. Select service, provider, location and a future slot | 3. Click Save
Test_Data: Service=General Medicine; Provider=Dr A Tester; Date=02-Jul-2026; Time=11:00
Expected_Result: Save is rejected with a validation message that a patient is required; no appointment record is created

Title: Booking requires a service to be selected
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; patient selected on Add new appointment screen
Test_Steps: 1. Select the patient | 2. Leave Service empty | 3. Select provider, location and a future slot | 4. Click Save
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic (ID=10001V); Provider=Dr A Tester; Date=02-Jul-2026; Time=11:30
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a required-field validation on Service; no appointment is created

Title: Prevent booking an appointment in the past
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; patient selected; current date is 01-Jul-2026
Test_Steps: 1. Select the patient | 2. Select service, provider and location | 3. Choose a date earlier than today | 4. Attempt to choose a time slot | 5. Click Save
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic (ID=10001V); Service=General Medicine; Date=29-Jun-2026; Time=09:00
Expected_Result: System prevents booking in the past with a clear validation message; no appointment is created

Title: Boundary - booking exactly at the current date and time edge
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; patient selected; server clock at 01-Jul-2026 09:00
Test_Steps: 1. Select patient, service, provider, location | 2. Choose today's date | 3. Select the earliest slot at or just after the current time | 4. Click Save
Expected_Result: Slot at or after current time is accepted and saved; any slot before current time is rejected

Title: Prevent double-booking the same provider in an overlapping time slot
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; provider Dr A Tester already has an appointment 10:00-10:30 on 02-Jul-2026
Test_Steps: 1. Select a different patient | 2. Select same provider and service | 3. Select date 02-Jul-2026 and an overlapping slot 10:15 | 4. Click Save
Test_Data: Patient=Mark Synthetic (ID=10002V); Provider=Dr A Tester; Date=02-Jul-2026; Time=10:15
Expected_Result: System detects the provider time conflict and blocks the save or warns; the overlapping appointment is not silently created

Title: Decision table - conflict handling across provider, location and patient overlaps
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; existing appointment 10:00-10:30 for Provider P1, Location L1, Patient Pt1
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt overlapping booking varying provider, location and patient as per data rows | 2. Observe whether conflict is raised for each combination | 3. Record the outcome
Test_Data: Rule1 sameProvider+sameTime=conflict; Rule2 sameLocation+sameTime+diffProvider=warn; Rule3 samePatient+sameTime=conflict; Rule4 allDifferent=allowed
Expected_Result: Each rule resolves to its expected outcome: provider and patient overlaps are blocked, location-only overlaps warn, and fully distinct bookings are allowed

Title: Prevent the same patient being booked into two overlapping appointments
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; patient already has appointment 10:00-10:30 on 02-Jul-2026
Test_Steps: 1. Select the same patient | 2. Select a different provider and service | 3. Choose an overlapping slot at 10:10 | 4. Click Save
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic (ID=10001V); Provider=Dr B Tester; Date=02-Jul-2026; Time=10:10
Expected_Result: System warns or blocks the overlapping patient appointment to avoid a patient being in two places at once

Title: Filter providers by selected service before booking
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; services mapped to specific providers
Test_Steps: 1. Select the patient | 2. Select a specialty service | 3. Open the provider dropdown | 4. Verify only providers offering that service are listed
Test_Data: Service=Cardiology; ExpectedProviders=Dr Cardio One, Dr Cardio Two
Expected_Result: Only providers associated with the selected service are shown; unrelated providers are not selectable

Title: Restrict bookable time slots to provider availability at the chosen location
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; provider has availability block 09:00-12:00 at Outpatient Clinic only
Test_Steps: 1. Select patient, service, provider | 2. Select location Outpatient Clinic | 3. View available slots | 4. Change location to Inpatient Ward | 5. View available slots
Test_Data: Provider=Dr A Tester; Loc1=Outpatient Clinic; Loc2=Inpatient Ward; Date=02-Jul-2026
Expected_Result: Slots 09:00-12:00 appear for Outpatient Clinic; no slots appear for a location where the provider has no availability

Title: Boundary - book the first and last available slot of an availability block
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; provider availability 09:00-12:00 with 30-min slots
Test_Steps: 1. Book the earliest slot at 09:00 | 2. Save | 3. Book the last slot at 11:30 | 4. Save | 5. Attempt to book 12:00
Expected_Result: First (09:00) and last (11:30) slots are bookable; the slot at the boundary end 12:00 outside the block is not offered or is rejected

Title: Reject booking when the requested duration exceeds the remaining availability
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; provider availability ends 12:00; service duration 60 min
Test_Steps: 1. Select patient, service (60 min), provider | 2. Select slot 11:30 | 3. Click Save
Test_Data: Service=Procedure 60min; Provider=Dr A Tester; Date=03-Jul-2026; Time=11:30
Expected_Result: Booking is rejected because a 60-minute service starting 11:30 exceeds the 12:00 availability end

Title: Reschedule an existing appointment to a new valid future slot
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; an appointment exists with status Scheduled on 02-Jul-2026 10:00
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient's appointment | 2. Click Edit/Reschedule | 3. Select a new future date and an available slot | 4. Click Save
Expected_Result: Appointment is updated to the new date and time, the original slot is freed, and history reflects the reschedule

Title: Reschedule into a conflicting slot is prevented
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; provider already booked 14:00-14:30 on 04-Jul-2026
Test_Steps: 1. Open an existing appointment | 2. Click Reschedule | 3. Select the provider's already-booked slot 14:00 | 4. Click Save
Test_Data: AppointmentID=APPT-5001; Provider=Dr A Tester; NewDate=04-Jul-2026; NewTime=14:00
Expected_Result: Reschedule is blocked or warned due to provider conflict; the appointment keeps its previous valid slot

Title: Reschedule of a completed appointment is not allowed
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; an appointment is in status Completed
Test_Steps: 1. Open the completed appointment | 2. Attempt to click Reschedule/Edit time
Expected_Result: Reschedule action is disabled or rejected for a completed appointment; the record remains unchanged

Title: Cancel a scheduled appointment with a cancellation reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; an appointment exists with status Scheduled
Test_Steps: 1. Open the appointment | 2. Click Cancel | 3. Select a cancellation reason | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Appointment status changes to Cancelled, the slot is released, and the cancellation reason and timestamp are recorded

Title: Cancellation requires a reason when configured as mandatory
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; cancellation reason configured as mandatory
Test_Steps: 1. Open a scheduled appointment | 2. Click Cancel | 3. Leave reason blank | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Cancellation is blocked with a validation requiring a reason; status remains Scheduled

Title: Mark a scheduled appointment as Missed/No-show after the slot passes
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; appointment scheduled for a slot that is now in the past and patient did not arrive
Test_Steps: 1. Locate the past scheduled appointment | 2. Open it | 3. Set status to Missed/No-show | 4. Save
Test_Data: AppointmentID=APPT-5004; Status from Scheduled to Missed
Expected_Result: Status transitions to Missed, the change is timestamped and attributed to the user, and the appointment is excluded from active queues

Title: Valid transition Scheduled to CheckedIn at patient arrival
Preconditions: Logged in as front-desk; appointment is Scheduled for today and patient has arrived
Test_Steps: 1. Open today's appointment list | 2. Select the patient's appointment | 3. Click Check In | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Status changes to CheckedIn with an arrival timestamp; appointment appears in the active clinical queue

Title: Valid transition CheckedIn to Completed after the visit
Preconditions: Logged in as provider; appointment is CheckedIn
Test_Steps: 1. Open the CheckedIn appointment | 2. Complete the visit workflow | 3. Set status to Completed | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Status transitions to Completed and the appointment is closed for further status edits except by privileged roles

Title: Invalid transition Completed back to Scheduled is rejected
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; appointment is in status Completed
Test_Steps: 1. Open the completed appointment | 2. Attempt to set status back to Scheduled
Expected_Result: Illegal state transition is rejected with a clear message; status remains Completed

Title: Invalid transition Cancelled to CheckedIn is rejected
Preconditions: Logged in as front-desk; appointment is Cancelled
Test_Steps: 1. Open the cancelled appointment | 2. Attempt to Check In the patient
Expected_Result: Check-in is blocked because a cancelled appointment cannot transition to CheckedIn; status remains Cancelled

Title: State transition coverage matrix for all defined statuses
Preconditions: Logged in with privileges to change status; test appointments in each base state
Test_Steps: 1. For each source state attempt every target state | 2. Record allowed vs blocked | 3. Compare with the defined state machine
Test_Data: States=Scheduled, CheckedIn, Completed, Cancelled, Missed; Allowed=Scheduled->CheckedIn/Cancelled/Missed, CheckedIn->Completed/Cancelled
Expected_Result: Only transitions defined in the state machine succeed; all undefined transitions are blocked consistently

Title: Schedule an appointment reminder for a future booking
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; reminder service enabled; patient has a valid contact method
Test_Steps: 1. Book a future appointment | 2. Enable reminder | 3. Select reminder lead time and channel | 4. Save
Expected_Result: A reminder job is scheduled for 24 hours before the appointment via the chosen channel and is visible in reminder logs

Title: Reminder is not sent for a cancelled appointment
Preconditions: A future appointment with a scheduled reminder exists
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel the appointment before the reminder lead time | 2. Wait until the reminder trigger time | 3. Check reminder dispatch log
Expected_Result: No reminder is dispatched for the cancelled appointment; the reminder job is voided in the log

Title: Reminder content excludes sensitive clinical details to protect PHI
Preconditions: Reminder channel configured; patient consented to SMS reminders
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a reminder for a booked appointment | 2. Inspect the delivered message content
Expected_Result: Reminder contains only minimum necessary information (date, time, location, clinic name) and no diagnosis or sensitive clinical detail

Title: Create a weekly recurring appointment series
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; recurring appointments enabled; provider availability covers the recurrence
Test_Steps: 1. Select patient, service, provider, location | 2. Choose recurrence Weekly | 3. Set start date and occurrence count | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Four weekly appointments are created on consecutive matching weekdays, each with its own ID and linked to the series

Title: Recurring series skips occurrences that conflict with provider availability
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; provider unavailable on one of the recurrence dates
Test_Steps: 1. Create a weekly recurring series spanning a date where the provider is off | 2. Save | 3. Review the generated occurrences
Expected_Result: Conflicting occurrence is skipped or flagged for manual resolution rather than silently overbooking

Title: Cancel a single occurrence without affecting the rest of the series
Preconditions: A recurring series of 4 appointments exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the second occurrence | 2. Cancel only this occurrence | 3. Confirm scope This appointment only | 4. Verify other occurrences
Expected_Result: Only the selected occurrence is cancelled; the remaining occurrences in the series stay Scheduled

Title: Cancel the entire future series from a chosen occurrence
Preconditions: A recurring series of 4 appointments exists; current occurrence is the second
Test_Steps: 1. Open the second occurrence | 2. Choose Cancel This and following | 3. Confirm
Expected_Result: Occurrence 2 and all later occurrences are cancelled while earlier completed/past occurrences remain unchanged

Title: Pairwise combinations of service, location, channel and patient type
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; multiple services, locations, reminder channels and patient types configured
Test_Steps: 1. Apply pairwise-selected combinations for booking | 2. Save each | 3. Verify booking and reminder behavior per combination
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination books successfully with the correct service, location and reminder channel applied; no invalid pairings are accepted

Title: Exploratory session on rapid status changes and concurrent edits
Preconditions: Two users with edit rights open the same appointment simultaneously
Test_Steps: 1. User A checks in the patient | 2. User B simultaneously cancels | 3. Observe final state and any errors | 4. Probe rapid repeated status toggles
Test_Data: AppointmentID=APPT-5008; Concurrent users A and B
Expected_Result: System resolves concurrency deterministically (last-write or lock), avoids corrupt state, and surfaces a clear conflict message; no orphaned slots

Title: Exploratory probe of timezone and DST handling around booking
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; server and client in different timezones; DST transition near booking date
Test_Steps: 1. Book an appointment near a DST change boundary | 2. View it as users in different timezones | 3. Verify displayed and stored times
Test_Data: Date near DST change; ServerTZ=UTC; ClientTZ=US/Eastern
Expected_Result: Stored time is unambiguous and displayed correctly per viewer timezone; no off-by-one-hour shift across DST

Title: Booking form is fully keyboard navigable and screen-reader labelled
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; screen reader available; on the Add new appointment screen
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate all booking fields using Tab/Shift+Tab only | 2. Operate dropdowns and date picker via keyboard | 3. Verify each control announces a label and state
Test_Data: Tools=NVDA/VoiceOver; Fields=Patient, Service, Provider, Location, Date, Time
Expected_Result: All controls are reachable and operable by keyboard, have programmatic labels, and announce required/invalid states per WCAG 2.1 AA

Title: Validation errors are announced and associated with their fields
Preconditions: On the booking form with screen reader active
Test_Steps: 1. Submit the form with missing required fields | 2. Verify error announcement | 3. Verify errors are programmatically tied to fields
Expected_Result: Each error is announced, focus moves to or references the first invalid field, and aria-describedby links the message to the control

Title: Unauthorized role cannot book or modify appointments
Preconditions: A user with a read-only or unrelated role is logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to open Add new appointment | 2. Attempt to cancel an existing appointment via UI and direct URL | 3. Observe access control
Expected_Result: Booking and modification actions are not available and direct attempts are rejected with an authorization error; no data is changed

Title: Appointment changes are written to the audit trail
Preconditions: Auditing enabled; logged in as Scheduler
Test_Steps: 1. Create, reschedule and cancel an appointment | 2. Open the audit log | 3. Verify each action is recorded
Expected_Result: Audit log records user, timestamp, action and before/after values for every appointment change

Title: Direct object reference to another patient's appointment is blocked
Preconditions: Logged in as a user scoped to a specific location/patient panel
Test_Steps: 1. Capture an appointment ID belonging to another restricted patient | 2. Request that appointment by ID via URL/API | 3. Observe response
Expected_Result: Access is denied for the out-of-scope appointment (no IDOR); response contains no PHI for the unauthorized record

Title: Booking input is sanitized against injection in free-text fields
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; booking form open
Test_Steps: 1. Enter script and SQL-like payloads in notes/reason fields | 2. Save | 3. Reopen and view the appointment | 4. Inspect rendering and storage
Expected_Result: Input is stored/escaped safely; no script executes on render and no query is altered; payload shown as inert text

Title: Create an appointment via the OpenMRS REST appointmentscheduling endpoint
Preconditions: Valid API session/token; patient, provider, service exist
Test_Steps: 1. POST to the appointment endpoint with a valid JSON body | 2. Capture the response | 3. GET the created appointment
Test_Data: Endpoint=POST /ws/rest/v1/appointmentscheduling/appointment; body includes patient, timeSlot, appointmentType, status=SCHEDULED
Expected_Result: API returns 201/200 with the new appointment UUID; a follow-up GET returns the persisted appointment with matching fields

Title: Reject API booking with a past time slot
Preconditions: Valid API token
Test_Steps: 1. POST an appointment with a timeSlot in the past | 2. Inspect status code and error body
Expected_Result: API rejects with a 4xx validation error and a descriptive message; no record is created

Title: Unauthenticated appointment API request is rejected
Preconditions: No valid session/token
Test_Steps: 1. Send GET/POST to the appointment endpoint without credentials | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: API responds 401 Unauthorized and returns no appointment or PHI data

Title: Pagination and filtering of appointments by date and provider
Preconditions: Valid API token; multiple appointments exist across dates
Test_Steps: 1. GET appointments filtered by provider and date range | 2. Verify pagination parameters | 3. Verify result set
Expected_Result: Only appointments matching the filter are returned, correctly paginated, with consistent ordering

Title: Create and read an Appointment resource via FHIR R4
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint available; Patient and Practitioner resources exist
Test_Steps: 1. POST a FHIR Appointment with status booked and participants | 2. Read it back by id | 3. Validate required fields
Test_Data: Resource=Appointment; status=booked; participant.actor=Patient/123 and Practitioner/456; start/end set
Expected_Result: FHIR server returns 201 with a resource id; the read-back Appointment conforms to R4 with correct status, participants and slot times

Title: FHIR Appointment status value set is enforced
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint available
Test_Steps: 1. POST a FHIR Appointment with an invalid status value | 2. Inspect the OperationOutcome
Expected_Result: Server rejects with an OperationOutcome citing an invalid code not in the Appointment status value set (proposed/pending/booked/arrived/fulfilled/cancelled/noshow/etc.)

Title: FHIR no-show maps to Appointment.status noshow
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint; an existing booked Appointment
Test_Steps: 1. PUT/PATCH the Appointment status to noshow | 2. Read back the resource
Expected_Result: Status updates to noshow and is consistent with the internal Missed/No-show state; participant required status remains valid

Title: Search FHIR Slot for free slots before booking
Preconditions: FHIR R4 Slot and Schedule resources configured for a provider
Test_Steps: 1. GET Slot?schedule=...&status=free&start=ge2026-07-02 | 2. Verify returned slots | 3. Book into a returned free slot
Expected_Result: Only free slots are returned; booking one transitions the Slot to busy and prevents it being offered again

Title: Inbound HL7 SIU S12 creates a new appointment
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine connected; SIU feed enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an SIU^S12 (new appointment) message | 2. Verify the appointment is created | 3. Verify the ACK
Test_Data: Message=SIU^S12; SCH segment with filler appt id; PID for Jane Synthetic; AIS service
Expected_Result: A matching appointment is created with mapped patient, service and time; an HL7 ACK (AA) is returned

Title: Inbound HL7 SIU S15 cancels an existing appointment
Preconditions: HL7 SIU feed enabled; an appointment exists matching the filler id
Test_Steps: 1. Send an SIU^S15 (appointment cancellation) referencing the existing appointment | 2. Verify status change | 3. Verify ACK
Test_Data: Message=SIU^S15; SCH filler id matches APPT-5009
Expected_Result: The referenced appointment is set to Cancelled and an ACK (AA) is returned; mismatched ids produce an application reject (AE)

Title: Malformed HL7 SIU message is rejected with a negative ACK
Preconditions: HL7 SIU feed enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an SIU message with a missing mandatory SCH field | 2. Inspect the ACK
Test_Data: Message=SIU^S12 with empty SCH appointment id
Expected_Result: Message is rejected with a negative ACK (AE/AR); no partial appointment record is created

Title: Cancelling an appointment frees its slot and updates status atomically
Preconditions: DB read access; an appointment occupies a slot
Test_Steps: 1. Record slot and appointment row before cancel | 2. Cancel via UI | 3. Query the appointment and slot tables
Test_Data: AppointmentID=APPT-5010; verify status column and slot occupancy
Expected_Result: Appointment row shows Cancelled with end-date set and the slot is released; both updates committed in a single transaction with no orphaned references

Title: Soft-deleted (voided) appointments are excluded from active queries
Preconditions: DB read access; an appointment has been voided
Test_Steps: 1. Void an appointment | 2. Run the standard active-appointment query | 3. Run a query including voided rows
Expected_Result: Voided appointment is excluded from active lists but retained in the database with voided flag, reason and auditor for traceability

Title: Referential integrity between appointment, patient and provider
Preconditions: DB access; an appointment exists
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect foreign keys on the appointment row | 2. Attempt to delete a referenced provider | 3. Observe constraint behavior
Expected_Result: Foreign keys reference valid patient and provider; deleting a referenced provider is blocked or handled per retire policy without orphaning the appointment

Title: Walk-in appointment created for today's immediate slot
Preconditions: Logged in as front-desk; patient present without prior booking
Test_Steps: 1. Select the patient | 2. Create an appointment for today's next available slot | 3. Immediately check in
Expected_Result: Appointment is created for today and can be checked in immediately, appearing in the active queue

Title: Booking honors patient appointment-type duration defaults
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; appointment types have distinct default durations
Test_Steps: 1. Select each appointment type | 2. Observe the auto-set duration and end time | 3. Save
Expected_Result: End time and slot length are set from the appointment type default and reflected on save

Title: Boundary - reschedule to the same slot leaves the appointment unchanged
Preconditions: An appointment exists at 02-Jul-2026 10:00
Test_Steps: 1. Open the appointment | 2. Click Reschedule | 3. Select the identical date and time | 4. Save
Expected_Result: No conflict is raised against itself; the appointment remains at the same valid slot and no duplicate is created

Title: Auto-flag no-show after grace period without manual action
Preconditions: No-show automation enabled with a grace period; appointment not checked in
Test_Steps: 1. Let a scheduled appointment pass its start plus grace period uncheck-in | 2. Run/await the no-show job | 3. Verify status
Expected_Result: After the grace period the appointment is automatically flagged Missed/No-show with an automated attribution in the audit log

Title: Reminder respects patient communication consent/opt-out
Preconditions: Patients with varied consent flags; reminders enabled
Test_Steps: 1. For each consent combination attempt a reminder | 2. Observe whether it dispatches per channel
Test_Data: Pt1 SMS=opt-in,Email=opt-out; Pt2 SMS=opt-out,Email=opt-in; Pt3 all opt-out
Expected_Result: Reminders dispatch only via consented channels; fully opted-out patients receive none; decisions are logged

Title: Maximum-length and special characters in appointment notes field
Preconditions: Booking form open; notes field present
Test_Steps: 1. Enter notes at the maximum allowed length | 2. Include valid special characters and Unicode | 3. Save and reopen
Test_Data: Notes length=max (e.g. 1000 chars); includes accents and emoji
Expected_Result: Notes are saved and displayed intact up to the max length; over-max input is truncated or rejected with a clear message

Title: Pairwise provider x service x location availability validation
Preconditions: Multiple providers mapped to services and locations with availability
Test_Steps: 1. Apply pairwise combinations of provider, service and location | 2. Check whether slots are offered | 3. Validate against configured mappings
Expected_Result: Slots are offered only for valid provider-service-location mappings; invalid combinations show no availability

Title: Check-in is blocked for an appointment scheduled on a future date
Preconditions: Logged in as front-desk; appointment scheduled for a future date
Test_Steps: 1. Open a future-dated scheduled appointment | 2. Attempt to Check In
Expected_Result: Check-in is prevented or warned for an appointment not due today; status remains Scheduled

Title: Cancel propagates slot release and reminder cancellation end-to-end
Preconditions: An appointment with a scheduled reminder and an occupied slot exists
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel the appointment with a reason | 2. Verify slot is freed | 3. Verify reminder voided | 4. Verify audit entry
Expected_Result: Status Cancelled, slot freed for rebooking, pending reminder voided, and a complete audit entry recorded across the chain

Title: End-to-end book then check-in then complete patient visit
Preconditions: Logged in users for scheduling, front-desk and provider roles; patient active
Test_Steps: 1. Book a future appointment | 2. On the day, check the patient in | 3. Provider completes the visit | 4. Verify final status and history
Expected_Result: Appointment progresses through all valid states with timestamps and attributions; final status Completed with full history retained

Title: Concurrent booking of the last free slot by two schedulers
Preconditions: Two schedulers attempt to book the same last remaining slot at the same time
Test_Steps: 1. Both users open the same free slot | 2. Both submit Save nearly simultaneously | 3. Observe outcomes
Expected_Result: Exactly one booking succeeds; the other is rejected with a slot-no-longer-available message; the slot is never double-booked

Title: Rescheduling preserves linkage to the originating recurring series
Preconditions: A recurring series exists; one occurrence will be moved
Test_Steps: 1. Open one occurrence of the series | 2. Reschedule it to a new valid slot | 3. Verify series linkage and other occurrences
Expected_Result: The moved occurrence keeps its series association, other occurrences are unaffected, and the series view reflects the new time

Title: Day, week and provider views render appointments consistently
Preconditions: Multiple appointments exist across providers and times for a given week
Test_Steps: 1. Open day view | 2. Switch to week view | 3. Switch to provider view | 4. Compare appointment counts and details
Expected_Result: All three views show the same appointments consistently with correct times, providers and statuses; no appointment is missing or duplicated

Title: FHIR Appointment requires start before end (invalid period rejected)
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint available
Test_Steps: 1. POST a FHIR Appointment with end earlier than start | 2. Inspect the OperationOutcome
Expected_Result: Server rejects the resource with an OperationOutcome indicating an invalid period (end before start); no resource is created

Title: Session timeout requires re-authentication before further booking changes
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; idle session timeout configured
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate and open a booking | 2. Remain idle beyond the timeout | 3. Attempt to save a change
Expected_Result: After timeout the action is blocked and the user is forced to re-authenticate; unsaved PHI is not exposed and no change persists without a valid session
